summary:
The condition results from a genetic mutation affecting the function or expression of LDL receptors, leading to high blood lipid concentrations characteristic of this form of familial hyperlipidemia. This impairment specifically disrupts normal hepatic processing of low-density lipoprotein, causing its excessive presence in plasma. \boxed{A}

The summary concisely captures the essential clinical point about how the disease manifests through an issue with LDL receptors while adhering strictly within two sentences as requested. It also maintains alignment with Agent2’s original choice for clarity regarding causality related to these specific molecular mechanisms involved directly in pathogenesis via inherited defects impacting cellular processes governing fat metabolism regulation at systemic level.
